location: Obtained by trading the Elden Remembrance (dropped after defeating Radagon of the Golden Order / the Elden Beast) to Enia, the Finger Reader, at Roundtable Hold.
tactics: This requires beating the game's final boss gauntlet, so it's effectively an end-of-playthrough or New Game+ weapon rather than something you can grab mid-run.

You can duplicate the Elden Remembrance once at a Wandering Mausoleum if you'd rather keep the original for a different reward, but only one Sacred Relic Sword is obtainable per Remembrance either way.
Its Wave of Gold weapon art costs a hefty 42 FP, so it's not something you can spam without real FP investment (Flasks of Wondrous Physick, high Mind).
Roundtable Hold is reached via its own dedicated Site of Grace fast-travel option, not by walking there from the open world.
lore: Forged from the remains of Radagon after his fusion with Marika, then taken up as a weapon by the Elden Beast itself. Its meaning is disputed in-world — some read it as a mark of ruin or grave sin, others see it as the close of one age and the dawn of the next.

builds.name: Faith/Dex Holy build
builds.why: D-scaling in both Dexterity and Faith with a large Holy damage split, plus the wide-hitting Wave of Gold weapon art for clearing groups.
